metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4015
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2B: Immunoelectron microscopy demonstrates DCV localisation in mossy fibre terminals. The synaptic contacts are marked by arrowheads while the dynorphin immunoreactivity concentrated on DCVs is marked by arrows.

Figure 3: Dynorphin labelled mossy fibre axon forms asymmetric synaptic contacts (marked by arrowheads) with dendritic spines. Dynorphin immunoreactivity concentrated on DCVs is marked by arrows. The synaptic vesicles can be used to compare for DCV morphology.

Figure 10: Subcellular distribution of dynorphin immunoreactivity in structures other than the axon terminal. A: Cytoplasm B: Dendrite C: Spines.

Note on Ab used: The cross-reactivity of this rabbit antiserum to dynorphin(9-17) was 100%, whereas, the cross-reactivity with dynorphin A(1-8), dynorphin A(1-13), dynorphin B, [leu]enkephalin, [Leu]enkephalin-Arg6, [Met]enkephalin, and alpha-neoendorphin was <0.1%. To check for specificity, control sections were processed with either omission of dynorphin antiserum or substitution of normal rabbit serum. However, the paper fails to add evidence for both cross-reactivity and specificity.. Experimental description: Surgical specimens from the hippocampal formation were obtained from seven patients with medically intractable Temporal Lobe Epilepsy. The individuals ranged in age from 23 to 42years (mean, 33.9 years) at the time of surgery. The age at the onset of epilepsy was 5 to 30 years (mean,13.9 years), and the duration of epilepsy was 3 to 36 years(mean, 20 years).
